title: 32nd Foundation Day Celebration
excerpt: Celebrating 32 years of academic excellence and character building at Eureka with awards for toppers, alumni, and teachers.
body:
Eureka Residential Secondary School celebrated its 32nd Foundation Day on 14th Poush (January). The event commenced with the hoisting of the school flag by Principal Mr. Kuran Chemjong, followed by a series of cultural performances, speech competitions, and science exhibitions.

During the official ceremony, the school honored the SLC/SEE toppers from past batches and presented Academic Excellence Awards to outstanding students of Grade X and XII. Long-serving teaching and non-teaching staff were also recognized for their dedication. The day concluded with a grand community feast for students, staff, and parents, highlighting the strong bond that defines the Eureka family.

title: Eureka LitArt Fest 2082
excerpt: A vibrant celebration of literature, art, drama, and creative storytelling showcasing the diverse talents of Eureka students.
body:
The annual LitArt Fest was organized in February to foster creative expression and artistic appreciation among Eureka students. The week-long festival featured painting contests, handwriting competitions, live poetry slams, and short play performances.

Students from Montessori to Grade XII actively participated in various clubs including the Readers' Club, Art Club, and Music Club to prepare displays and acts. The highlight of the festival was the 'Young Artists Gallery' where students showcased their paintings, crafts, and robotic AI-based artwork models. The festival closed with an interactive story writing workshop led by famous local writers.

title: Winter Camp "Know Your Root"
excerpt: An immersive cultural and heritage camp connecting students with Eastern Nepalese traditions, languages, and community history.
body:
Eureka's Winter Camp 'Know Your Root' was successfully conducted this year during the winter break. The camp's central objective was to expose students to local cultures, Eastern Nepalese history, and traditional arts.

Students visited historical and cultural spots in Dharan, participated in traditional dance classes, and learned about traditional cooking. Guest lecturers spoke about values, positive discipline, and community leadership. The camp also integrated yoga and sports sessions to maintain physical health and mental focus.
